And so, I'm sure you get asked this, how do you compare to Lulu in your, I mean, obviously it's an interesting question, because I think when it comes down to, like, some fabrics, we actually share some fabrics, because once you look behind the curtain, there's, there's, like, three mills that make all the athletic fabric in the world, like anything else.
Like, I don't know if you guys know this, but, like, energy tabs and bars and stuff, a lot of them are all made, like, in the same factories and stuff.
It's not quite that close for us, but, so I think from a product standpoint, I mean, obviously their core is running, their core is yoga, ours is running.
So, there you have some, like, functionality differences to account for, but I would say a couple of differences.
One is just the activity of yoga and the activity of running have, they're almost like different life philosophies.
Like, I appreciate and respect yoga, because it's about kind of being present in your body and being connected to your mind and your body and to kind of the bigger, bigger world.
And then, but running, especially people who like to train and like to race, it's more about, like, it is more about community, because you don't go to a yoga class to catch up with your friends.
I mean, you might do it afterwards or beforehand, but with running, there's that whole talking and running at the same time.
So, that's how relationships and community get built.
And then, the other thing is just this goal setting thing.
Like, running is about, like, you put a race on the calendar, and it might be, like, three months away, but you work towards that goal.
And you know that there's going to be ups and downs, and you might get injured, and it's going to hurt, and in that race, you know, you're going to be afraid, but you're going to push through.
And so, for me, running has just always been more about, like, real life, like, because it has all of those ingredients.
And it's outdoors, like, I just feel like we spend too much time plugged into, you know, computers and devices and inside, and I'm actually, like, a real purist.
I have a Garmin and all that stuff, like, when I'm training, I can pace myself, but my favorite thing is to be outside with no technology and just breathe, you know.
